sections_cited: Section 21 of The Punjab Rented Premises Act
Section 2l of The Punjab Rented Premises Act

BLUE SLIP 4.J0 C-41 (Revised under Acr No.I[ or !990) ORDER OF THE COURT In the Court Tribunal: Hon ble Mr Justice Miza Rau Case No Mrit Petition No 1IS cfZOLg Iled ت Zatar لقعوا and another Additional District Iudge Raualpinفi Cap at Kahسal and كعط@ Decided on: 1u 020كl8 Judgment approved for reporting Registrar should see with reference the work conducl Ul some Judicial Officer, Ves No 1{`$0, separale conridential nole should be sent to him in question Fes No CERIIFICATE Certified the Judement order i8rا ٨ ٧٥٥ t٥une #tc5 principle Iءl١ decid؛٤ q0٠51١00 Whiich Iirsl impressiun disunguishes ٨٨٨٤٣ ٤ ٢ ١٦ ٠ ٧ ٢ ٠٦ ١ ٢١١ ،١٥٨؛٧٥٤١ ؛١٥٧ ٢٧٦| fudgets ) Strike out whichever is not applicable NoIc: This slip is only to be used when some action is to be taken If the slip is used, the Court Associate must attach it on top of first page of the judgment Court Associate must ask the Judge the Jucgment whether the judgment ts approved for reporting Those directions which are not to be used should be deleted o طe اau b Lla Judgmenit Upload on Website Entry in Database Natne: Signature Dated ٧٩ Ve5 ورتدادھ ات writing Form No.HCJDC٠liI ORDER SHEET IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT RAWALPINDI BENCH RAWALPINDI IUDICIAL DEPARTMENT Writ Petitiun No.175 0f 2018 Zafar Iqbal and anotner Versus Additional District Judge Rawalpindi (Camp at Kahuta) and Olhers $..N0 ١٢٠٢ Datc ٥٨٥٢ Ordtr wifli stgnaturc of Judie unu thnt D٢ parties ،١ ، ٠ ١ ١؛»٧٧ Proceedin! gسا3فشە اآی41 ١٢٦،؛٨٧٥٤٦ 14.05.2018 Mr Zulfiqar M Kiani, Advocate for the petitioners Raja Muhammad Aslam, Advocate for responoent N٥.3 Through instant petition under Aricle 199 of Ihe Consuitution or The Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, Ihe petitioners assail the vires of judgment dated 13 November, 2017, Whereby Ihe learned Additional District Judge Rawalpindi Camp at Kahuta, while dismissing their appeal affirmed the order dated 22 " April, 2OIT passed by the learned Special Judge (Rent) Kahuta District Rawalpindi, resulting into an order of eviction of the petitioners frotn the suit premises The facts in brief necessary for adjudication of instant petition are that respondent ٧٥. (hereinafier referred `respondent ) moved an application seeking ejecument of the petitioners fromn shop situated Mator Road Kahuta averring therein that Ihic petitioners have obtained the from the "respondent ` on rent but have failed to lender rent since May, 2015 ejectment petition was allowed by way of order dated April, ZUIT and Ihe petitioners were directed to hand over the vacanit possession Of the suit Within IwO months respondent", ٨٦٥ ٦ was also held entitled for the recovery of arrears of rent Rs OOO - from May, 2Ol5 till eviction of the petitioners The petitioners feeling dissatisfied from the said orcer said 5h10p thcy Ihe 22" 500p

gسا3فشە اآی41 ١٢٦،؛٨٧٥٤٦ 14.05.2018 Mr Zulfiqar M Kiani, Advocate for the petitioners Raja Muhammad Aslam, Advocate for responoent N٥.3 Through instant petition under Aricle 199 of Ihe Consuitution or The Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, Ihe petitioners assail the vires of judgment dated 13 November, 2017, Whereby Ihe learned Additional District Judge Rawalpindi Camp at Kahuta, while dismissing their appeal affirmed the order dated 22 " April, 2OIT passed by the learned Special Judge (Rent) Kahuta District Rawalpindi, resulting into an order of eviction of the petitioners frotn the suit premises The facts in brief necessary for adjudication of instant petition are that respondent ٧٥. (hereinafier referred `respondent ) moved an application seeking ejecument of the petitioners fromn shop situated Mator Road Kahuta averring therein that Ihic petitioners have obtained the from the "respondent ` on rent but have failed to lender rent since May, 2015 ejectment petition was allowed by way of order dated April, ZUIT and Ihe petitioners were directed to hand over the vacanit possession Of the suit Within IwO months respondent", ٨٦٥ ٦ was also held entitled for the recovery of arrears of rent Rs OOO - from May, 2Ol5 till eviction of the petitioners The petitioners feeling dissatisfied from the said orcer said 5h10p thcy Ihe 22" 500p فللغستنتآ٧ though preferred an Appa berore the learned Addilion3l District Judge Rawalpindi Camp at Kahuta but the samne was dismissed by way of impugned judgment dated 13 November, 2017, Learned counsel for the petitioners submitted that the petition before the Civil Court was not competent and to this elfect preliminary objection was raised He added that the leamed Civil Judge proceed with the matter while the ejectment petition as suit Learned counsel contended Iniai the petitioners were never Servcd requeU UnCCI Section 21 of The Punjab Rented Premises Act, 2009, |1 15 emnphatically that the ejecument order was passed without adhering the mandatory provisions of law, which is coram nOnl judice Learned counsel emphasiced that even the leared Additional District Judge has failed to apply judicial Inind the facts of the case and the 1|35 dismissed in an Illegal and unlawful manner Ihe contrary, learned counsel for ' respondent" vehemently defended the impugned judgment He added that eviction UrUCr w45 passed after fulfilling all Ife c008/ formalities, Learned counsel contended that the petitioners have failed IO move an application IOF leave COfNeSt, Which WaS Inandatory for resisting the ejectment petition Learned counsel CIHIDIaSIZ`٥ Ihat there CUIICUICIII findings, which are based on proper appraisal of evidence 8nd consuitutional JUTI5٥Ict٥n uniwaianted UnUCr Ihe CICUInSIanCCS After having heard learned counsel for both the sides considerable length, have also perused record inl order Io properly appreciate their respective conitentions The application seeking eviction of the petitioners was filed on 15" March, 2016, which was entertained by the learned Civil Judge as suit for possession and accordingly trenting argucd appeal
